lawtotal.label: Firearm law count (Tufts)
lawtotal.definition: Sum of 72 binary firearm law indicators coded by Siegel et al. for the Tufts State Firearm Laws database. Higher values mean more restrictive policy.

law_bg_check_index.label: Background check index
law_bg_check_index.definition: Number of Tufts background-check-related provisions in force, including universal/handgun-only checks, gun-show checks, state-level checks, and ammunition checks.

law_carry_restriction_index.label: Carry restriction index
law_carry_restriction_index.definition: Sum of restrictions on concealed and open carry, including may-issue concealed carry, permit requirements for concealed carry, and open carry restrictions for handguns and long guns.

law_banned_weapons_index.label: Banned-weapons index
law_banned_weapons_index.definition: Sum of bans on assault weapons (general, list-based, one-feature), restrictions on grandfathered ownership, large-capacity magazine bans, and 10-round limits.

law_buyer_restriction_index.label: Buyer restriction index
law_buyer_restriction_index.definition: Sum of permit-to-purchase, registration, age-21 sale restrictions, lost/stolen reporting, one-gun-per-month limits, and waiting periods.

law_dv_index.label: Domestic-violence prohibitor index
law_dv_index.definition: Sum of domestic-violence prohibitor laws covering misdemeanor crimes of domestic violence (MCDV), surrender requirements, ex parte orders, DVRO/stalking provisions, and incident-based removal.

law_storage_cap_index.label: Storage / GVRO / liability index
law_storage_cap_index.definition: Sum of locked-storage / child-access prevention, dealer liability, and gun violence restraining order (GVRO / red-flag) provisions.

magazine.label: LCM sale ban (binary)
magazine.definition: 1 if the state bans the sale of large-capacity magazines (LCMs) beyond just assault-pistol ammunition. The threshold for what counts as "large capacity" varies by state; see the magazine_threshold_handgun and magazine_threshold_long_gun variables for the round count.

magazinepreowned.label: LCM possession ban (binary)
magazinepreowned.definition: 1 if the state additionally prohibits possession of pre-owned large-capacity magazines (i.e. magazines acquired before the state's LCM sale ban took effect). 0 means owners may keep grandfathered LCMs even if new sales are banned. This is a stricter form of LCM regulation: California adopted an LCM possession ban in 2017 (Prop 63), New Jersey lowered its threshold and eliminated grandfathering in 2018, and DC's LCM ban has always covered possession.

magazine_threshold_handgun.label: LCM round-count threshold (handguns)
magazine_threshold_handgun.definition: Round-count threshold above which a handgun magazine is classified as a "large-capacity magazine" under the state's LCM sale ban. Most banning states use 10 rounds (CA, CT, MA, MD, NY, NJ, OR, WA, HI, DC); Colorado uses 15 (HB13-1224, 2013); Vermont uses 15 for handguns (2018); Illinois uses 15 for handguns (PICA, 2023); Delaware uses 17 (HB 450, 2022). NaN for state-years without an active LCM sale ban. Hand-coded from public state-by-state summaries (Giffords Law Center, RAND Smart Policy) — see data/processed/lcm_thresholds_state.csv. Note: the threshold shown is the CURRENT statute and is broadcast across all ban years; states whose threshold shifted mid-period (e.g. NJ 15->10 in 2018) display the current value.
magazine_threshold_handgun.source: Hand-coded from Giffords / RAND state-by-state LCM summaries
magazine_threshold_handgun.caveat: Current-statute snapshot broadcast to all ban years; threshold changes within a state are not time-resolved.

magazine_threshold_long_gun.label: LCM round-count threshold (long guns)
magazine_threshold_long_gun.definition: Round-count threshold above which a long-gun magazine is classified as a "large-capacity magazine" under the state's LCM sale ban. Most banning states match their handgun threshold; Vermont (10 long-gun vs. 15 handgun) and Illinois (10 long-gun vs. 15 handgun under PICA) use a stricter limit for rifles. Hawaii's LCM ban applies to handgun magazines only — long-gun magazines are unrestricted, so this column is NaN for HI. NaN for state-years without an active LCM sale ban. Same source and current-statute caveat as magazine_threshold_handgun.
magazine_threshold_long_gun.source: Hand-coded from Giffords / RAND state-by-state LCM summaries
magazine_threshold_long_gun.caveat: Current-statute snapshot broadcast to all ban years; threshold changes within a state are not time-resolved. Hawaii's LCM ban does not cover long-gun magazines (NaN).

assault.label: Assault weapons ban (binary)
assault.definition: 1 if state bans the sale of assault weapons beyond assault pistols.

assaultlist.label: AWB by named-list definition (binary)
assaultlist.definition: 1 if the state's assault-weapons ban includes an explicit list of named/banned models (e.g., AR-15, AK-47 variants). Most states with an AWB use a list approach.

onefeature.label: AWB by one-feature definition (binary)
onefeature.definition: 1 if the state's assault-weapons ban uses a one-feature test: any semi-automatic rifle with a detachable magazine PLUS one military-style feature (pistol grip, telescoping stock, flash hider, etc.) qualifies as an assault weapon. Combined with a low-round LCM ban, this regime effectively forces semi-automatic rifles into fixed-magazine, bolt-action, or single-shot configurations. CA, CT, IL, NY, WA, MA, NJ, DE use this test.

assaultregister.label: Grandfathered AWs must be registered (binary)
assaultregister.definition: 1 if owners of pre-existing (grandfathered) assault weapons must register them with the state. A more restrictive AWB feature.

assaulttransfer.label: Transfer of grandfathered AWs prohibited (binary)
assaulttransfer.definition: 1 if grandfathered assault weapons cannot be sold or transferred (only kept by the original registered owner). Among the most restrictive AWB features.

fixed_magazine_regime.label: Effective fixed-magazine regime (binary, derived)
fixed_magazine_regime.definition: 1 if the state has all three: an assault-weapons ban (assault), a one-feature definition (onefeature), and a large-capacity-magazine sale ban (magazine). The combination effectively forces semi-automatic rifles to be configured with fixed magazines, switched to bolt-action / lever-action / pump / single-shot, or sold elsewhere. This is the closest analogue in U.S.\ law to a 'detachable-magazine ban' for rifles. Derived from Tufts variables; not an independent statute.
fixed_magazine_regime.source: Derived from Tufts State Firearm Laws (assault, onefeature, magazine)

gvro.label: Red-flag / GVRO law (binary)
gvro.definition: 1 if state has a gun violence restraining order / extreme risk protection order law allowing temporary firearm removal.

universal.label: Universal background checks (binary)
universal.definition: 1 if the state requires a background check on every firearm transfer, INCLUDING private sales between individuals (gun shows, online, person-to-person). 0 means only federally-licensed dealers (FFLs) must run NICS checks; private sales between residents are exempt under federal law unless a state statute closes that gap.

permitconcealed.label: Permit required to carry concealed (binary)
permitconcealed.definition: 1 if the state requires a state-issued permit to carry a concealed firearm in public. 0 means "permitless carry" (sometimes called "constitutional carry") — any adult who can legally possess a firearm may carry it concealed without first obtaining a permit. The opposite of this variable, when it switches from 1 to 0, is the operational definition of "adopted permitless carry" used in the project's causal analyses.

mayissue.label: May-issue concealed carry (binary)
mayissue.definition: 1 if the state's concealed-carry permitting authority has DISCRETION over whether to issue a permit ("may-issue" / "good cause required"), or if the state outright bans concealed carry. 0 means "shall-issue" — the authority MUST issue a permit to any applicant who meets objective statutory criteria. The 2022 NYSRPA v. Bruen Supreme Court decision made many remaining may-issue regimes unconstitutional, so this variable is mostly historical post-2022.

violent_rate.label: Violent crime rate
violent_rate.definition: FBI UCR violent crime offenses per 100,000 population. Includes murder/nonnegligent manslaughter, rape, robbery, and aggravated assault.
violent_rate.source: FBI Uniform Crime Reports / OpenCrime extraction

property_rate.label: Property crime rate
property_rate.definition: FBI UCR property crime offenses per 100,000 population. Includes burglary, larceny-theft, and motor vehicle theft.
property_rate.source: FBI Uniform Crime Reports / OpenCrime extraction

homicide_rate.label: Homicide rate
homicide_rate.definition: Murder and nonnegligent manslaughter per 100,000 population (FBI UCR / OpenCrime).
homicide_rate.source: FBI Uniform Crime Reports / OpenCrime extraction

robbery_rate.label: Robbery rate
robbery_rate.definition: Reported robbery offenses per 100,000 population (FBI UCR / OpenCrime).
robbery_rate.source: FBI Uniform Crime Reports / OpenCrime extraction

rape_rate.label: Rape rate
rape_rate.definition: Reported rape offenses per 100,000 population (FBI UCR / OpenCrime). Definition broadened in 2013; pre/post-2013 levels are not directly comparable.
rape_rate.source: FBI Uniform Crime Reports / OpenCrime extraction
rape_rate.caveat: FBI revised the rape definition in 2013; the pre-2013 series uses the legacy definition.

aggravated_assault_rate.label: Aggravated assault rate
aggravated_assault_rate.definition: Reported aggravated assault per 100,000 population (FBI UCR / OpenCrime).
aggravated_assault_rate.source: FBI Uniform Crime Reports / OpenCrime extraction

burglary_rate.label: Burglary rate
burglary_rate.definition: Reported burglary offenses per 100,000 population (FBI UCR / OpenCrime).
burglary_rate.source: FBI Uniform Crime Reports / OpenCrime extraction

larceny_rate.label: Larceny rate
larceny_rate.definition: Reported larceny-theft per 100,000 population (FBI UCR / OpenCrime).
larceny_rate.source: FBI Uniform Crime Reports / OpenCrime extraction

motor_vehicle_theft_rate.label: Motor vehicle theft rate
motor_vehicle_theft_rate.definition: Reported motor vehicle theft per 100,000 population (FBI UCR / OpenCrime).
motor_vehicle_theft_rate.source: FBI Uniform Crime Reports / OpenCrime extraction

firearm_suicide_rate.label: Firearm suicide rate
firearm_suicide_rate.definition: Firearm suicides per 100,000 population, derived from the firearm-suicide-share dataset (Kalesan-style v2 file) for 1949-2023.
firearm_suicide_rate.source: Firearm suicide / homicide v2 dataset (Kalesan supplement style)

total_suicide_rate.label: Total suicide rate
total_suicide_rate.definition: All-method suicides per 100,000 population, derived from the firearm-suicide-share dataset for 1949-2023.

firearm_homicide_rate.label: Firearm homicide rate
firearm_homicide_rate.definition: Firearm homicides per 100,000 population from the firearm-suicide-share v2 file.

ownership_rand.label: Gun ownership rate (RAND HFR)
ownership_rand.definition: RAND State-Level Household Firearm Ownership Rate (HFR), 1980-2016. Schell et al. structural equation model combining BRFSS, GSS, Pew, hunting licenses, Guns & Ammo subscriptions, NICS, and the Fem/Male firearm-suicide-share into a single estimate of the share of adults living in a household with any firearm. Most-cited modern state ownership measure; ends in 2016.
ownership_rand.caveat: Series ends in 2016. Standard errors are non-trivial in early years.

ownership_fss.label: Gun ownership proxy (FS/S)
ownership_fss.definition: Firearm-suicide-share proxy for state household gun ownership: firearm suicides / all suicides. Most-used long-run proxy in the literature, but mechanically correlated with suicide outcomes; do NOT use as a regressor when the outcome is suicide.
ownership_fss.caveat: Mechanically correlated with suicide outcomes. Treat as descriptive ownership proxy only.

nics_total_per_100k.label: NICS background checks per 100k (market activity)
nics_total_per_100k.definition: Annual FBI NICS background checks per 100,000 population. A flow proxy for legal market activity, NOT a stock measure of gun owners. Background checks do not equal gun sales (multiple firearms per check; some states use NICS for permit re-checks). Do not interpret as ownership rate.
nics_total_per_100k.source: FBI NICS via Data Liberation Project
nics_total_per_100k.caveat: Checks are not sales; some states submit large permit re-check volumes that distort year-over-year comparison.

share_white_nh.label: Share non-Hispanic white
share_white_nh.definition: Population share identifying as non-Hispanic white. Reconstructed from Census ASRH (1990-1999), intercensal (2000-2009), and Census/PEP (2010-2024) files; ACS 1-year 2008-2024 in the modern panel.
share_white_nh.source: Census Bureau historical population estimates and ACS

share_black_nh.label: Share non-Hispanic Black
share_black_nh.definition: Population share identifying as non-Hispanic Black. Same reconstruction approach as share_white_nh.
share_black_nh.source: Census Bureau historical population estimates and ACS

share_hispanic.label: Share Hispanic
share_hispanic.definition: Population share identifying as Hispanic or Latino, any race.
share_hispanic.source: Census Bureau historical population estimates and ACS

share_male.label: Share male
share_male.definition: Population share male. Reconstructed from Census ASRH/intercensal/PEP files.
share_male.source: Census Bureau historical population estimates and ACS

share_age_15_24.label: Share age 15-24
share_age_15_24.definition: Population share aged 15-24, the high-violence-risk demographic window typically used in the gun-policy literature.
share_age_15_24.source: Census Bureau historical population estimates and ACS

share_age_25_44.label: Share age 25-44
share_age_25_44.source: Census Bureau historical population estimates and ACS

share_bachelors_plus.label: Share with bachelor's degree or higher (25+)
share_bachelors_plus.definition: Share of adults 25+ with a bachelor's degree or higher. Anchored on Census Statistical Abstract Table 229 (1990, 2000, 2008) and ACS 1-year 2008-2024; pre-ACS years are linearly interpolated within state.
share_bachelors_plus.caveat: Pre-2008 values are interpolated within state from anchor years.

median_hh_income_real_2024.label: Median household income (real 2024 USD)
median_hh_income_real_2024.definition: Median household income from Census SAIPE, deflated to 2024 dollars with CPI-U. SAIPE 1990-1992 and 1994 missing values are interpolated within state.
median_hh_income_real_2024.source: Census SAIPE deflated to real 2024 USD
median_hh_income_real_2024.caveat: 1990-1992 and 1994 values were interpolated within state.

poverty_rate.label: Poverty rate
poverty_rate.definition: Census SAIPE all-ages poverty rate. Same interpolation caveat as income.

unemployment_rate.label: Unemployment rate
unemployment_rate.definition: BLS state unemployment rate (annual average of monthly LAUS).

pcpi_real_2024.label: Per-capita personal income (real 2024 USD)
pcpi_real_2024.definition: BEA per-capita personal income, deflated to 2024 dollars with CPI-U.

population.label: Population
population.definition: Annual state resident population from FRED / Census.

imprisonment_rate.label: Imprisonment rate
imprisonment_rate.definition: Total prisoners under state jurisdiction (year-end stock) per 100,000 residents. Source: BJS National Prisoner Statistics. DC is NaN post-2001 (Lorton closed; DC prisoners transferred to BOP). Pre-1996 has gaps for years where the BJS PDF reports were not in the legacy archive (1981, 1984, 1993-1995).

police_expenditure_per_capita_real_2024.label: Police protection expenditure per capita (real 2024 USD)
police_expenditure_per_capita_real_2024.definition: State + local government combined expenditure on Police protection, per capita, deflated to 2024 dollars using BLS CPI-U. Source: Census Annual Survey of State and Local Government Finances. Linearly interpolated within state across 2003-2008 and 2012-2016 gap years; pre-2002 and 2023+ remain blank.
police_expenditure_per_capita_real_2024.source: Census Annual Survey of State & Local Government Finances

has_death_penalty.label: Death penalty (active statute)
has_death_penalty.definition: 1 if the state had an active death-penalty statute on December 31 of the year; 0 otherwise. Source: hand-coded from the published timeline of state abolitions and reinstatements (DPIC). Coded as statute-on-the-books, NOT moratoria-adjusted (so CA and PA remain coded 1 despite Newsom and Wolf moratoria).
has_death_penalty.source: Death Penalty Information Center (timeline of abolitions)

executions_count.label: Executions in year
executions_count.definition: Count of state-jurisdiction executions in this state-year (federal executions excluded). Source: DPIC executions database. 0 for state-years with no execution recorded.

sworn_officers_per_100k.label: Sworn officers per 100,000 (state rollup)
sworn_officers_per_100k.definition: Full-time sworn (peace-officer-status) law-enforcement personnel summed across all reporting agencies in the state, divided by state population. Source: Kaplan LEOKA (openICPSR project 102180 V15) aggregated agency-level rows. Federal LE (FBI, DEA, ATF, US Marshals) and special-jurisdiction agencies (Amtrak, transit) excluded because they have no county FIPS.

alcohol_per_capita_ethanol_gallons.label: Alcohol per capita (ethanol, gallons / year)
alcohol_per_capita_ethanol_gallons.unit: Gallons of ethanol per resident age 14+ per year
alcohol_per_capita_ethanol_gallons.definition: NIAAA apparent per-capita ethanol consumption — sum of beer + wine + spirits ethanol equivalents in gallons, divided by state resident population age 14+. The canonical alcohol-availability proxy in gun-policy research (used by McClellan-Tekin 2017, Luca-Malhotra-Poliquin 2017, Koper-Roth 2001, Crifasi 2015). Source: NIAAA Alcohol Epidemiologic Data System / openICPSR project 105583 V5. Coverage 1977-2018; later years require the next openICPSR vintage.

drug_overdose_per_100k.label: Drug overdose mortality (per 100,000)
drug_overdose_per_100k.definition: CDC NCHS Drug Poisoning Mortality, model-based death rate, aggregated from county-year (population-weighted) to state-year. Captures the opioid-era confounder that any post-2010 firearm-policy spec needs to control for (per McCourt 2020 suicide stack). Source: CDC NCHS NCHS-Drug-Poisoning-Mortality-by-County (data.cdc.gov/rpvx-m2md). Coverage 2003-2021.
drug_overdose_per_100k.source: CDC NCHS Drug Poisoning Mortality by County

religion_adherents_pct_2020.label: Religious adherents as % of population (2020)
religion_adherents_pct_2020.definition: Share of state population reported as adherents of any religious group in the 2020 US Religion Census (Religious Congregations and Membership Study). 2020 cross-section broadcast across all years (slow-moving baseline; the 1980/1990/2000/2010 censuses are paywalled at ARDA). Used as a suicide-spec covariate per Crifasi 2015, Kivisto-Phalen 2018, McCourt 2020.

synthetic_opioid_death_rate.label: Synthetic opioid (fentanyl) death rate
synthetic_opioid_death_rate.definition: State-year synthetic-opioid (fentanyl-included) overdose death rate per 100,000, drawn from CDC Vital Statistics Rapid Release / Multiple Cause of Death using ICD-10 underlying cause T40.4. The post-2014 fentanyl-era surge nearly fully accounts for the rise in total drug-overdose deaths through 2022. Coverage 2015--2024 here (the underlying data file is zero-filled pre-2015, but those cells are treated as missing on the map because the synthetic-opioid epidemic was negligible before then). Used as a deaths-of-despair control alongside BRFSS frequent mental distress and NSDUH any-mental-illness prevalence.
synthetic_opioid_death_rate.source: CDC VSRR / NCHS Multiple Cause of Death (T40.4)

freq_mental_distress_pct.label: Frequent mental distress (BRFSS)
freq_mental_distress_pct.unit: % of adults reporting >=14 poor mental-health days in past 30
freq_mental_distress_pct.definition: State-year prevalence of frequent mental distress, defined as adults reporting >=14 of the past 30 days when their mental health was not good. From the CDC Behavioral Risk Factor Surveillance System (BRFSS) Healthy Days module. Coverage 2019--2024 here (the BRFSS Healthy Days module dates back to 1993, but the build script only pulled the post-2019 prevalence-trend release; pre-2019 cells are zero-filled in the file and treated as missing on the map). Used as a deaths-of-despair control alongside synthetic-opioid death rate and NSDUH any-mental-illness prevalence.

ami_pct.label: Any mental illness prevalence (NSDUH)
ami_pct.definition: State-year prevalence of any mental illness (AMI) in the past year among adults, from SAMHSA's National Survey on Drug Use and Health. NSDUH publishes state estimates as two-year rolling averages; we assign each estimate to the upper year. Coverage 2016--2024 here (older NSDUH state-SAE releases were not pulled; pre-2016 cells are zero-filled in the file and treated as missing on the map). NSDUH's two-year-rolling-average release schedule produces gap years even within the coverage window. Used as a deaths-of-despair control alongside synthetic-opioid death rate and BRFSS frequent mental distress.
